trans-unit.source: AppHost
trans-unit.target: AppHost

trans-unit.source: Could not determine AppHost version
trans-unit.target: Could not determine AppHost version

trans-unit.source: AppHost version {0} ({1})
trans-unit.target: AppHost version {0} ({1})
trans-unit.note: {0} is the AppHost Aspire.Hosting version. {1} is the AppHost project path.

trans-unit.source: Could not determine AppHost version ({0})
trans-unit.target: Could not determine AppHost version ({0})
trans-unit.note: {0} is the AppHost project path.

trans-unit.source: Aspire
trans-unit.target: Aspire

trans-unit.source: (channel: {0})
trans-unit.target: (channel: {0})
trans-unit.note: {0} is the Aspire channel name (e.g., "stable", "staging", "daily", "local", "pr-1234"). Appended to CLI / AppHost version messages.

trans-unit.source: Checking Aspire environment...
trans-unit.target: Verifica dell'ambiente Aspire in corso...

trans-unit.source: Aspire CLI version {0}
trans-unit.target: Aspire CLI version {0}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the current Aspire CLI version.

trans-unit.source: Run '{0}' to update Aspire CLI.
trans-unit.target: Run '{0}' to update Aspire CLI.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the command to update Aspire CLI.

trans-unit.source: Aspire CLI version {0} is out of date. Latest version is {1}
trans-unit.target: Aspire CLI version {0} is out of date. Latest version is {1}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the current Aspire CLI version. {1} is the latest available Aspire CLI version.

trans-unit.source: Could not check for Aspire CLI updates
trans-unit.target: Could not check for Aspire CLI updates

trans-unit.source: Channel
trans-unit.target: Channel

trans-unit.source: Path
trans-unit.target: Path

trans-unit.source: PATH status
trans-unit.target: PATH status

trans-unit.source: Route
trans-unit.target: Route

trans-unit.source: Version
trans-unit.target: Version

trans-unit.source: Container Runtime
trans-unit.target: Runtime del contenitore

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) API server returned HTTP {0} ({1}).
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) API server returned HTTP {0} ({1}).
trans-unit.note: {0} is the numeric HTTP status code. {1} is the HTTP status code name.

trans-unit.source: The running Aspire CLI is not associated with a bundle layout that contains the Developer Control Plane (DCP).
trans-unit.target: The running Aspire CLI is not associated with a bundle layout that contains the Developer Control Plane (DCP).

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) bundle not found; skipping connection health checks
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) bundle not found; skipping connection health checks

trans-unit.source: Failed to check Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health
trans-unit.target: Failed to check Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health checks succeeded
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health checks succeeded

trans-unit.source: Timed out after {0} seconds while checking Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health.
trans-unit.target: Timed out after {0} seconds while checking Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the timeout in seconds.

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using the developer certificate failed
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using the developer certificate failed

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using the developer certificate succeeded
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using the developer certificate succeeded

trans-unit.source: The developer certificate could not be cached because it was not valid.
trans-unit.target: The developer certificate could not be cached because it was not valid.

trans-unit.source: The developer certificate did not have a thumbprint.
trans-unit.target: The developer certificate did not have a thumbprint.

trans-unit.source: No fully trusted exportable developer certificate with a private key was found.
trans-unit.target: No fully trusted exportable developer certificate with a private key was found.

trans-unit.source: Run `aspire certs trust` to repair developer certificate trust.
trans-unit.target: Run `aspire certs trust` to repair developer certificate trust.

trans-unit.source: Run `aspire certs trust` to create and trust a developer certificate.
trans-unit.target: Run `aspire certs trust` to create and trust a developer certificate.

trans-unit.source: No trusted developer certificate was available for the Developer Control Plane (DCP) developer certificate connection check
trans-unit.target: No trusted developer certificate was available for the Developer Control Plane (DCP) developer certificate connection check

trans-unit.source: The developer certificate could not be cached because the user profile directory could not be determined.
trans-unit.target: The developer certificate could not be cached because the user profile directory could not be determined.

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using an ephemeral DCP-managed certificate failed
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using an ephemeral DCP-managed certificate failed

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using an ephemeral DCP-managed certificate succeeded
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using an ephemeral DCP-managed certificate succeeded

trans-unit.source: Expected Developer Control Plane (DCP) executable at '{0}'.
trans-unit.target: Expected Developer Control Plane (DCP) executable at '{0}'.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the expected DCP executable path.

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) executable not found
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) executable not found

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) exited before writing kubeconfig. Exit code: {0}.{1}{2}
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) exited before writing kubeconfig. Exit code: {0}.{1}{2}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the DCP process exit code. {1} is a newline. {2} is recent DCP process output.

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) kubeconfig did not contain a valid server URI.
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) kubeconfig did not contain a valid server URI.

trans-unit.source: Developer Control Plane (DCP) did not write any output.
trans-unit.target: Developer Control Plane (DCP) did not write any output.

trans-unit.source: Failed to start Developer Control Plane (DCP).
trans-unit.target: Failed to start Developer Control Plane (DCP).

trans-unit.source: TLS authentication failed: {0}
trans-unit.target: TLS authentication failed: {0}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the TLS authentication error message.

trans-unit.source: Diagnose Aspire environment issues and verify setup
trans-unit.target: Diagnostica i problemi dell'ambiente Aspire e verifica la configurazione

trans-unit.source: For detailed prerequisites: {0}
trans-unit.target: For detailed prerequisites: {0}
trans-unit.note: {0} is a clickable URL

trans-unit.source: Run '{0}' to remove all certificates, then run '{1}' to create and trust a new one.
trans-unit.target: Run '{0}' to remove all certificates, then run '{1}' to create and trust a new one.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the aspire certs clean command, {1} is the aspire certs trust command (not localizable)

trans-unit.source: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion.
trans-unit.target: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion.

trans-unit.source: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates), but none are trusted
trans-unit.target: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates), but none are trusted

trans-unit.source: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion when selecting which one to use.
trans-unit.target: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion when selecting which one to use.

trans-unit.source: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates)
trans-unit.target: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates)

trans-unit.source: Aspire uses HTTPS for secure communication between the dashboard and your services during local development.
trans-unit.target: Aspire uses HTTPS for secure communication between the dashboard and your services during local development.

trans-unit.source: No HTTPS development certificate found
trans-unit.target: No HTTPS development certificate found

trans-unit.source: Certificate {0} exists in the personal store but was not found in the trusted root store.
trans-unit.target: Certificate {0} exists in the personal store but was not found in the trusted root store.

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate is not trusted
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate is not trusted

trans-unit.source: Older certificate versions (< v{0}) may not support all certificate trust scenarios.
trans-unit.target: Older certificate versions (< v{0}) may not support all certificate trust scenarios.

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate has an older version ({0})
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate has an older version ({0})

trans-unit.source: The certificate is in the trusted store, but SSL_CERT_DIR is not configured to include '{0}'. Some applications may not trust the certificate. 'aspire run' will configure this automatically.
trans-unit.target: The certificate is in the trusted store, but SSL_CERT_DIR is not configured to include '{0}'. Some applications may not trust the certificate. 'aspire run' will configure this automatically.

trans-unit.source: Set SSL_CERT_DIR in your shell profile: {0}
trans-unit.target: Set SSL_CERT_DIR in your shell profile: {0}

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate is only partially trusted
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate is only partially trusted

trans-unit.source: Run '{0}' to trust the HTTPS development certificate.
trans-unit.target: Run '{0}' to trust the HTTPS development certificate.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the aspire certs trust command (not localizable)

trans-unit.source: [trusted]
trans-unit.target: [trusted]

trans-unit.source: [partial]
trans-unit.target: [partial]

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate is trusted
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate is trusted

trans-unit.source: Environment
trans-unit.target: Ambiente

trans-unit.source: Aspire Environment Check
trans-unit.target: Verifica ambiente Aspire

trans-unit.source: Aspire CLI Installations
trans-unit.target: Aspire CLI Installations

trans-unit.source: Install discovery failed. See the Aspire CLI logs for details.
trans-unit.target: Install discovery failed. See the Aspire CLI logs for details.

trans-unit.source: Output format (Table or Json)
trans-unit.target: Esporta i risultati in formato JSON per l'integrazione con gli strumenti

trans-unit.source: Run 'aspire init' (or any aspire run/add/update/pipeline command) to migrate to aspire.config.json. The legacy file continues to work — this is a non-blocking warning.
trans-unit.target: Run 'aspire init' (or any aspire run/add/update/pipeline command) to migrate to aspire.config.json. The legacy file continues to work — this is a non-blocking warning.

trans-unit.source: Legacy .aspire/settings.json detected at {0}
trans-unit.target: Legacy .aspire/settings.json detected at {0}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the full path to the legacy settings file.

trans-unit.source: Operating system: {0}
trans-unit.target: Operating system: {0}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the operating system display name and version, for example "Windows 10.0.22631", "macOS 26.0", or "Linux Ubuntu 24.04".

trans-unit.source: .NET SDK
trans-unit.target: .NET SDK

trans-unit.source: Summary: {0} passed, {1} warnings, {2} failed
trans-unit.target: Riepilogo: {0} superati, {1} avvisi, {2} non superati

trans-unit.source: (current)
trans-unit.target: (current)
trans-unit.note: Suffix appended to the running CLI's path so the user can identify which install is themselves.

trans-unit.source: (not probed)
trans-unit.target: (not probed)
trans-unit.note: Placeholder shown in the table when an install was found but not asked to self-describe (e.g., trust gate).

trans-unit.source: active
trans-unit.target: active
trans-unit.note: Shown when an install is the first Aspire CLI resolved from PATH.

trans-unit.source: not on PATH
trans-unit.target: not on PATH
trans-unit.note: Shown when an install was not discovered from PATH.

trans-unit.source: shadowed
trans-unit.target: shadowed
trans-unit.note: Shown when an install is on PATH but an earlier Aspire CLI entry shadows it.

trans-unit.source: (probe failed)
trans-unit.target: (probe failed)
trans-unit.note: Placeholder shown in the table when an install was probed but did not return usable data.

trans-unit.source: (unknown)
trans-unit.target: (unknown)
trans-unit.note: Placeholder shown in the table when a field could not be determined for a discovered install.

trans-unit.source: unknown
trans-unit.target: unknown
